claimBoundary: This is an exhaustive repository-evidence CLAIM INVENTORY across four canonical system-chain source families. It is NOT universal CVF end-to-end proof, NOT a live/provider/runtime execution record, and does not promote, create, or select any T1-T4 live case. It performs zero live, provider, browser, business-CLI, runtime, or CI-job invocation. Every claim retains full source-item provenance; duplicate merging was conservative and found zero duplicate groups.

claimDedupe.mergeRationale: Conservative normalization per work order Required Inventory Method step 5: owner, edge, boundary, and asserted behavior must all match before merging. An automated signature check (owner + edge/boundary + first-80-chars of asserted behavior) found zero duplicate groups across all 99 claims, so every source item remains its own claim key with full 1:1 provenance. This is the safe default when in doubt per the work order's over-merging warning.

description: The matrix row claim for GC-011 is disposed PROVEN (UC-03 coverage-ledger current-runtime receipt, PASS_TWO_OF_TWO_WITH_RECEIPT_CASE_IDENTITY_LIMITATION), while the catalog EDGE entity citing the same control (cvf.asc.edge.gc011_pipeline_orchestrator.v1) is disposed STATIC_NOT_APPLICABLE because its own asserted behavior is a narrower, explicitly sampledOnly, HISTORICAL_TRACE caller-existence citation that its own claimBoundary forbids treating as full invocation proof.
contradictions.resolution: Not a true contradiction: the two claims have different owners, different asserted behaviors, and different evidence classes by design. The matrix-row claim inherits the UC-03 current-run receipt; the catalog-edge claim is a static, sampled, historical citation that intentionally does not inherit that receipt. Both dispositions are correct for their own narrow scope and are kept separate rather than merged, per the conservative claim-key rule.

knowledgeSystemReconciliation.crossRegionLinks: each claim's contributingSourceItemIds links back to exactly one sourceItemLedger row; map/interlock/catalog claims also cross-reference the coverage ledger by laneId/useCaseId/controlId where an exact-scope match exists
knowledgeSystemReconciliation.driftCheck: PASS: source file hashes recomputed at generation time match the values captured during the read pass
knowledgeSystemReconciliation.rebuildabilityCheck: PASS: the generator script deterministically re-derives sourceItemLedger and claims from the four source files plus the coverage ledger; rerunning it against the same commit reproduces the same 99 rows and dispositions
knowledgeSystemReconciliation.retrievalBoundary: this inventory can answer which claim keys exist, their disposition, and their exact source locator; it cannot answer new semantic questions not already recorded in the four source files or coverage ledger without a fresh governed review
knowledgeSystemReconciliation.adversarialVerification: recomputed mapped=99/deferred=0/unmapped=0 independently from the claims array length and cross-checked against the sourceItemLedger length (both 99)
